node.text:
The 2026 Special Olympics USA Games are coming to Minnesota from June 20-26. 
 
The Games will feature 16 dynamic sports, including the debut of Pickleball and Cornhole, and more than 3,000 incredible Special Olympics and Unified athletes. 
 
As a co-presenting partner of the 2026 Special Olympics USA Games, UnitedHealthcare is proud to celebrate the spirit of teamwork and inclusivity!

node.text: When Bobby needed emergency heart surgery, a UnitedHealthcare advocate coordinated the next steps so that Bobby could focus on healing. Learn more about Bobby at the link in our bio.

node.text:
Brandi’s journey to motherhood is something she brings into her work as a UnitedHealthcare clinical coordinator and registered nurse who helps members with high-risk pregnancies, those in the neonatal intensive care unit and in postpartum.

“I’m very nurturing when it comes to my members,” she said. “I do feel like I have that maternal instinct and bond with them. Everyone needs a mom in their life.”

It’s that compassion that Brandi leads with that helps her connect with members on another level, where they feel safe to ask for support.

“To them, we’re more than an insurance company,” she said, “they feel like we’re family.”

See how she leads with compassion to support at-risk moms.

node.text: As a college student athlete, MiMi is always on the move. Her UnitedHealthcare plan gives her coverage and confidence when she needs health care far from home. Learn more about Mimi's story at the link in our bio.

node.text: The best ideas start by listening. Using member feedback, Marta leads the development of the UnitedHealthcare app which helps millions of members manage their health care every day. Learn more about Marta at the link in our bio.

node.text: After retirement, Dawn’s life changed with a rare cancer diagnosis. With compassionate support and quick approvals from UnitedHealthcare, she could focus on healing. Learn more about Dawn at the link in our bio.

node.text:
The Hometown Kid 🏠
 
Brock takes us through his OT winner celly and what growing up playing hockey in Minnesota has meant to him.
 
Wild fans, now it’s your turn. Show us your best celly, with #ShowMeYourCelly for a chance to win a suite and stay tuned for Matt Boldy next! 👀

node.text:
🧱THE WALL OF ST. PAUL!
 Listen as Wally explains his iconic celly and the impact it brings to the fans and team.
 
Wild fans, now it’s your turn. Show us your best celly, with #ShowMeYourCelly for a chance to win a suite and stay tuned for Brock Faber next! 👀

node.text:
UnitedHealthcare and the Minnesota Wild want to see your best hockey cellys. Share your favorite celly on Instagram for a chance to win a suite at a Wild game. 
 
Let’s see ‘em, State of Hockey. 
 
#ShowMeYourCelly | @unitedhealthcare

node.text:
When life changed unexpectedly, UnitedHealthcare helped Tom and his wife find affordable health coverage—and peace of mind.

Learn more about Tom's story at the link in our bio.

node.text: Choosing the right health insurance, even when you're young and healthy, may help save you money if something unexpected comes up. Learn more at the link in our bio.

Centenarians have wisdom worth sharing, and the numbers speak for themselves:

-The #1 key to emotional resilience? A strong support system.

-Nearly 4 in 5 spend time with family or friends every week.

-27% are even turning to AI for answers!

How else are they keeping it 100? Find out at the link in our bio.

node.text:
Extra pressure can creep into the holiday season and test even the jolliest person.
 
As you decorate, shop, and attend social obligations, it’s normal to feel like stress during the holiday season.
 
Here are 5 mindful tips to help tackle holiday stress.
 
1. Set a realistic budget and stick to it. If money is tight, find low- and no-cost ways to show appreciation for the people in your life.
2. Self-care can often become deprioritized, exacerbating feelings of stress. Giving yourself even 15 minutes to unwind may help you recharge and feel better.
3. A healthy lifestyle may also help you make it through the holiday rush. This time off from school or work can be the perfect opportunity to develop healthy habits.
4. For a mental health boost, consider digital resources available through your health plan.
5. Whether you’re feeling stressed or burned out, or managing ongoing mental health concerns, recognizing the challenges and addressing them early on can help make the holiday season feel more balanced, fulfilling and joyful.
 
Remember, many people experience stress during the holidays. The tips above may help make this a less stressful season.

Centenarians today feel 11 years younger than those who hit 100 a decade ago. What’s their secret? Humor, optimism and staying active. Discover more insights from the 100@100 study by clicking the link in our bio.

node.text:
Suleima, a nurse practitioner with HouseCalls, meets with UnitedHealthcare Medicare members in their homes as part of their annual HouseCalls benefit.

Through the HouseCalls benefit, Suleima answers questions, assesses their environment and helps support their health care journey. That includes helping them feel comfortable by speaking their language.

When Suleima visits members like Angela, she’s able to do everything from check her blood pressure, assess her home for fall prevention and go over her current medications.

“I felt happy when I saw Suleima speaking to me in Spanish and treating me as if we had known each other for a long time,” Angela said, through translation.

“What makes me proud to be a HouseCalls provider is meeting people where they’re at,” Suleima said. “I want to be out on a medical mission every day. That for me is so important. That’s what I love about this specialty of nursing.”

node.text:
Heart. Determination. Inclusion.

@drake.maye teams up with @unitedhealthcare to support Team Massachusetts ahead of the 2026 @specialolympics USA Games for My Cause My Cleats!

Centenarians share their secret to longevity: keep moving, keep thinking and keep connecting. From daily walks to puzzles and conversations, the 100at100 Study reveals timeless habits for a healthy mind and body.

Get more centenarian advice at the link in our bio.

node.text:
When UnitedHealthcare Dual Complete member Tommye began experiencing worsening health conditions in her 60s, she knew it was time to find support.
 
“I didn’t have health problems when I was younger. And it seems like as I’ve evolved and aged, you need the help,” Tommye said. “I want to take the best care of myself. I want to keep the machine running for as long as I can.”
 
Through her UnitedHealthcare Dual Complete plan — a Dual Special Needs (D-SNP) plan for people with Medicare and Medicaid — she’s been able to get just that. 
 
“It feels like I have quality of life and I can be on my own.”

node.text: This is what living past 100 really looks like, and if you ask Charlotte, it’ll be here before you know it. Click the link in our bio to learn more from centenarians. #100at100

node.text:
HouseCalls advanced care practitioners (APCs) are united in their passion for service and helping UnitedHealthcare Medicare Advantage members live healthier, richer lives.

What is the impact of a HouseCalls visit?

In 2024 alone, HouseCalls addressed over 3 million gaps in care for UnitedHealthcare Medicare Advantage members. Moreover, 75% of these members had an in-office primary care visit within 90 days of a HouseCalls visit.

Each HouseCalls APC is driven to provide empathetic care in all of these circumstances and beyond.

“The social aspect of our job is really important because I think a lot of times, members are very apprehensive to let people in their house,” said Tyanna Montijo, a HouseCalls APC who sees members in a mix of urban, rural and tribal areas in Arizona. “Maybe they don't really like talking to people. So I use it as an opportunity to just sort of say, ‘Hey, you know, we're in your home. These are your rules. I'm just going to be asking you some questions. Tell me what you feel comfortable answering.’”

We just released a refreshed look at our legacy study of 100-year-olds. We found they’re staying active, gathering with loved ones, and living their most authentic lives.

See how else these centenarians are keeping it 100 at the link in our bio.

node.text:
Michael Venton II, manager of community engagement and outreach at Health Plan of Nevada Medicaid, believes a boots-on-the-ground approach is critical to building trust and serving the needs of local residents.

“We’re all local. Even from my days as a community health worker, we always emphasized that we’re local, so we can stay abreast of the changes in the community,” Michael said. 

“Every day you make somebody’s day. Not everybody can say that.”

See how Michael helps support local communities through his work.

node.text:
After trying out Surest for a year, Ari said she’s never switching: “It’s changed the way I feel about health insurance.” 

Surest (@suresthealth) is a health insurance plan that offers upfront pricing and no deductibles. It’s designed to make health care simpler, and allow members to make more informed choices by comparing costs up front, seeing provider Care Ratings and checking coverage details – all of which can be done at your fingertips, through the Surest app.
 
“Surest is a health care company that puts the patient in the driver’s seat,” Ari said. “It allows me to make my own decisions with transparency and visibility prior to selecting a doctor.”

node.text:
When Julia needed extra care during her pregnancy, Care Cash helped eliminate the stress of covering the costs.
 
But what is Care Cash? And how does Care Cash work?
 
Care Cash is a preloaded debit card that can be used to help pay for eligible medical expenses. Julia used her Care Cash card at her doctor’s office and never even saw a bill for the ultrasound.
 
“It gave me peace of mind in a scary situation, when it would have been tough to pay out of pocket at that time,” she said.
 
As she continues her pregnancy journey and prepares to welcome her baby in the fall, Julia is reminded how even small supports — like covering one unexpected ultrasound — can make a big impact.
 
“It’s been a huge lifesaver,” she said, “not having to worry about the whole cost of everything and being able to just focus on remaining healthy and growing a healthy baby.”

node.text:
First-time parents Jake and Mackenzy know all too well the excitement, and stress, of planning for a new baby. 
 
Welcoming their son this last year meant preparing for a big change to their lives full of unpredictability. But thanks to their Surest health plan, the one thing they could predict was the cost of delivery and care.
 
Surest (@suresthealth) is a health plan designed to simplify care by removing deductibles and offering clear, upfront pricing. Surest members can compare costs, review provider ratings, and check coverage details before care.
 
As Jake said "You're not going to get any surprises."

node.text:
When you’re looking at health plans options, don’t forget about specialty benefits. 

Some health plans may feature other programs designed to improve your well-being and help you maximize the value of your benefits. 

UnitedHealthcare plans may offer perks like vision benefits, dental plans, hearing plans, and more. 

Review your plan during Open Enrollment to see if you have access to reward programs, hearing assessments, dental coverage, ways to save on health care costs, and more.

node.text:
This year’s open enrollment season is a good chance to review how you’re using health services.

When reviewing your potential health plan, consider what mental health services does the plan cover? Are extra benefits such as dental, vision, hearing, and critical illness coverage included in your plan? Are wellness incentives included in your plan?

You also might be asking, what is Open Enrollment? Or when is Open Enrollment?

An open enrollment period is a window of time that happens once a year — typically in the fall — when you can sign up for health insurance, adjust your current plan or cancel your plan. It’s usually limited to a few weeks. If you miss it, you may have to wait until the next open enrollment period to make any changes.

If you get health insurance through your job, through Medicare, or through the Affordable Care Act (ACA) marketplace, you will have an open enrollment period.

Make sure you have all your health needs covered for 2026.

node.text:
When choosing a health plan for 2026, it's a good idea to look at what's new or changing. Eligible UnitedHealthcare members may have access to new digital tools to simplify searching for care or expanded maternal health support. 
 
Eligible members may also have access to programs that help cover out-of-pocket health care expenses, or rewards programs that may offer incentives for completing certain daily health program goals. 

Learning what's new for Open Enrollment may help you feel confident you're covered for what lies ahead.

node.text: Migraines can manifest in a number of ways, including sensory changes in hearing, vision or speech called migraine auras. See the link in bio.

node.text:
Heather needed additional support to navigate her health care needs and live more independently. 

“Before Jennifer, I was losing hope. Every step I took, I had to jump through many hoops before I could just get the basic, most simple things”

As a member of UnitedHealthcare Community Plan of Tennessee, Heather is eligible to participate in a long-term services and support program (LTSS). These services make it possible for individuals to receive additional support in their own home instead of a care facility.

Heather's plan is a Dual-eligible Special Needs plan or DSNP. DSNPs are specialized Medicare Advantage plans that provide healthcare benefits for beneficiaries that have both Medicare and Medicaid coverage. 

See how a care coordinator through her helps Heather live a healthier life.

node.text: Meet Natharan who appreciates her yearly HouseCalls visit to help manage her health. "It's kind of unusual to get that much time with a healthcare provider...it's personal," she said. Learn more at the link in bio.

node.text: Many people may not know what statins are until they have a prescription, but it’s never too early to pay attention to cholesterol. Learn more at the link in bio.

node.text:
Jennifer’s son Dane has complex health needs. That made the thought of switching health plans during open enrollment overwhelming. But after moving to Surest, she was left thinking, “what’s the catch?” 

Surest (@suresthealth) is a health plan designed to make navigating care easier by providing upfront pricing and eliminating deductibles. Members can make more informed decisions by using the Surest app to compare costs, see provider ratings and check coverage details. 

See how Surest transformed care for their family.

node.text:
If you’ve never purchased health insurance on your own and don’t deal with many health issues, it may be tempting to forgo coverage to save on premiums. But that may be short-sighted.
 
Health insurance coverage can help protect you from sudden illness and emergencies, provide access to preventive care, and help you avoid tax penalties while helping you save money. Sometimes, this can even come with a health savings plan, or HSA. 
 
While you don’t plan to get sick or hurt, medical expenses without insurance can be costly.

node.text: Finding common interests among family members of different ages can be challenging, but the emotional rewards are well worth the effort. Learn more at the link in our bio.

node.text:
UnitedHealthcare volunteers built and distributed the new bikes — complete with a helmet, bike pump and lock — for children with the nonprofit Communities for People, whose mission is to support children and families in the foster care system.

Communities for People provides a wide range of supportive programs and services for foster care families, plus assistance in developing independent living skills for those aging out of foster care.

node.text:
Minnesota was buzzing with energy, heart, and inspiration. 

@specialolympicsma flew out to join @sominnesota for a joint practice, right after the @patriots and @vikings wrapped up theirs. The field was alive with teamwork, inclusion, and unforgettable moments.

Guest coaches Drake Maye and Harrison Phillips brought full big-league coaching energy, leading their teams with passion and purpose.

As presenting partner of the 2026 Special Olympics USA Games, UnitedHealthcare is proud to create experiences that empower athletes and build lasting memories. #2026USAGames #playunified #specialolympics #inclusionrevolution

node.text:
Thank you for saying yes to helping kids and changing lives through the UnitedHealthcare Children's Foundation.
 
The UnitedHealthcare Children's Foundation aims to award over 3,500 medical grants each year to families across the United States.
 
Donations from individuals, corporations, and fundraising events make it possible for more kids to live happier, healthier lives.

node.text: What makes HouseCalls special? It’s a blend of clinical rigor and human connection at home. See how HouseCalls practitioners make an impact on the members they serve each year.

node.text: Every day, UnitedHealthcare advocate Gary listens with compassion to help members improve their health.
To help your child start the school year healthy and ready, be sure to schedule a visit with their doctor for a wellness exam and screenings. Learn more at the link in our bio.

node.text: A UnitedHealthcare Children’s Foundation grant helps a young girl with a rare disease grow and develop.
Mental health needs show up differently across kids at every stage. Learn the signs and ways to support as a parent. Find more information at the link in bio.

node.text: A UnitedHealthcare Children’s Foundation grant helped fund Noah’s hearing aids, which helped him grow his confidence — at home and at school. Link in bio.
